cataloging_subject.label: Palladius on Husbondrie
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged subject is the anonymous Middle English verse translation of Palladius's Latin De re rustica, including its authorial prohemium and thirteen books. DIMEV indexes the prohemium separately as record 254 and the main poem as record 1071, but the dedication manuscript and Liddell's represented edition present them as one designed work. The manuscript's alphabetical Tabula is associated paratext, while Liddell's title page, appended collation, list of variations, and errata are modern edition layers; all are accounted in the source structure without being flattened into medieval authorship, work language, form, or subject facets.

catalog_summary.title: Palladius on Husbondrie
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous Middle English poet-translator
catalog_summary.date: Translated by 1444, probably close to production of the 1439-1444 dedication manuscript; exact composition date unresolved
catalog_summary.region: England; exact place of translation and dialect region unestablished

identifiers.value: MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2, fols. xix r-xxi v and 1r-118v

titles.value: The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica

scope.description: Exact title of Mark Liddell's represented 1896 edition

titles.value: Middle English verse translation of Palladius's De re rustica
Agriculture as in nature and art / Tendure, of creature al Creatour
notes: The first phrase is a decorated manuscript line and is encoded by Liddell/U-M as a HEAD rather than an L element.

agents.name: Mark Harvey Liddell
notes: Internet Archive metadata says 'Marc Harvey'; the printed title page says Mark Liddell and governs this record.

agents.name: Thomas Norton
notes: Proposed as translator by David Howlett; the current Bodleian catalog calls the proposal interesting but says authorship is uncertain.
Palladius is the author of the Latin source work, not of the Middle English wording or prohemium.
A reported scholarly proposal, not a normalized known authorship.

scope.description: Immediate 1896 source edition and its modern paratext

notes: Edition-level responsibility only.

date_statements.display: By 1444; probably close to production of the 1439-1444 dedication manuscript
notes: The current catalog securely dates manuscript production from a reference to Humfrey's Oxford donations. The dedication-copy context supports a 1444 terminus ante quem for the translation, but no inspected authorial datum proves 1439 as its terminus post quem.

date_statements.display: 1439-1444 in Bodleian Library MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2
1873-1879 in Barton Lodge's Early English Text Society edition from MS. Add. A. 369
notes: The represented Duke Humfrey d. 2 edition is Liddell's later 1896 publication.

places.label: England
notes: Broad origin only; neither precise translation place nor dialect region is established by the inspected current evidence.
The current catalogs place both analyzed witnesses in England. Oxford is named in the prohemium as the recipient of Humfrey's books and is not silently promoted to a translation place.

language_statements.label: Middle English
notes: Latin rubrics and glosses belong to witness and editorial representation; modern English notes and apparatus belong to Liddell's edition. Neither layer makes the abstract translated work multilingual.

form_statements.description: A verse translation predominantly in seven-line ababbcc rhyme-royal stanzas. The sixteen-stanza prohemium uses eight-line ababbcbc stanzas. Four main-text LG elements each combine two seven-line stanzas, and Book XIII's eight-line verse table is encoded as TABLE rows rather than L elements.

subject_statements.label: Seasonal rural practice
notes: Books II-XIII are organized from January through December; Book I treats foundational conditions and management.

summaries.value: An anonymous fifteenth-century Middle English verse translation of Palladius's late-antique agricultural treatise. An authorial prohemium dedicates the work to Humfrey, Duke of Gloucester; Book I treats air, water, land, buildings, and estate management, while Books II-XIII arrange agricultural tasks month by month. The poem covers soils, crops, vines, orchards, gardens, livestock, bees, farm structures, processing, storage, and preservation. CME00114 represents Mark Liddell's 1896 edition from the dedication manuscript now Bodleian MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2, corrected and collated against Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369, with Liddell's appended comparison to Barton Lodge's earlier EETS edition.

extent.description: Encoding metrics and modeled leaves: seven DIV1, sixty-six DIV2, 227 DIV3 (300 divisions total), 934 LG, 6,605 L, 292 PB, 2,015 NOTE, nineteen P, 1,002 ITEM, and one TABLE with eight ROW. Of the ITEM elements, 1,001 are content: 499 in the manuscript Tabula, 464 in the collation Tabula, and 38 in the errata; one additional ITEM is in TEMPHEAD/REVDESCR/CHANGE and is revision metadata. The nineteen counted content leaves below are the title page, manuscript Tabula, prohemium, thirteen books, collation Tabula, list of variations, and errata. Raw L count understates represented verse by nine positions: the decorated first prohemium line is a HEAD and Book XIII's eight-line verse table is encoded in eight ROW elements. Four fourteen-line LG elements each combine two seven-line stanzas. The represented verse therefore has 6,614 line positions and 939 stanza/table units, while viewer metrics preserve the auditable XML counts of 6,605 L and 934 LG.

content_parts.label: Liddell edition title page
content_parts.summary: The modern title, editor, Part I—Text statement, Berlin publisher, and 1896 date.

locators.value: PDF p. 7; XML DIV1[1], PB REF 4

content_parts.label: Alphabetical Tabula
content_parts.summary: The dedication manuscript's extensive alphabetical subject index, transmitted before the prohemium and transcribed by Liddell.

locators.value: fols. x r-xviii v; complete IIIF canvases 21-38
DIV1[2], PB REF 10-27; 499 ITEM in 22 alphabetical DIV2 parts

notes: Associated manuscript paratext, not a second abstract work.

content_parts.label: Prohemium: Agriculture as in Nature and Art
content_parts.summary: A sixteen-stanza authorial prohemium invoking divine aid, addressing Humfrey, Duke of Gloucester, and framing the agricultural translation.

locators.value: fols. xix r-xxi v; complete IIIF canvases 39-44
DIV1[3], PB REF 28-32; 16 LG and 127 L

notes: The decorated opening line is HEAD[2], so the sixteen eight-line stanzas contain 128 represented line positions although only 127 are L elements.
DIMEV record 254 indexes this prohemium separately; it remains an integral part here.

content_parts.label: Thirteen-book verse translation
content_parts.summary: The complete verse translation, beginning with agricultural foundations and continuing through twelve month-books from January to December.
notes: The current Bodleian foliation restarts at 1 after the prohemium; Liddell's legacy foliation numbers this run from 13.

content_parts.label: Book I: Aier, Watir, Lond, and Gouernaunce
content_parts.summary: Foundations of husbandry: air, water, land, buildings, estate disposition, livestock, tools, gardens, vines, and related management.

content_parts.label: Book XIII: Decembre
content_parts.summary: Agricultural tasks for December and the poem's final devotional epilogue.

locators.value: DIV1[4]/DIV2[13], 7 chapters; DIV2/@N repeats 12 although HEAD says XIII

notes: Eight additional verse positions are encoded as a TABLE between numbered positions 70 and 79; the represented book has 86 verse positions.

content_parts.label: Collation with MS. Bodl. Add. A. 369: Tabula
content_parts.summary: Liddell's appended collation of the EETS edition with Bodleian MS. Add. A. 369, beginning with its Tabula variants.

locators.value: DIV1[5], PB REF 268-278; 464 ITEM in 19 DIV2 parts

notes: Modern scholarly apparatus, not part of the medieval work.

content_parts.label: List of variations by book
content_parts.summary: Book-by-book readings and manuscript differences continuing Liddell's Add. A. 369 collation.

locators.value: DIV1[6], PB REF 279-298; twelve DIV2 book headings and thirteen P

notes: The source edition's list has twelve DIV2 book divisions because Add. A. 369 lacks Book XIII.

content_parts.label: Errata
content_parts.summary: Thirty-eight corrections to Liddell's text, notes, collation, and punctuation.
notes: The cached Internet Archive scan ends after printed p. 289 and lacks these two unnumbered source pages; their exact exemplar remains unresolved.

related_works.title: De re rustica
notes: The late-antique Latin agricultural source work is distinct from the anonymous Middle English verse translation.

evidence.locator: IDG, TEMPHEAD, seven DIV1 layers, complete descendants
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for repository identity, exact title page, encoded work and apparatus boundaries, headings, notes, page milestones, lines, stanzas, indices, variations, and errata.

evidence.citation: Reproducible structural and page-boundary audit of CME00114.xml against Liddell's scan and the complete Duke Humfrey d. 2 IIIF object.
evidence.locator: Element counts; DIV hierarchy; LG-length distribution; Book XIII TABLE; PB REF crosswalk; first and final lines
evidence.summary: Confirms 777,936 bytes; seven DIV1, 66 DIV2, 227 DIV3, 934 LG, 6,605 L, 292 PB, 2,015 NOTE, nineteen P, 1,002 ITEM (1,001 content ITEM plus one revision-description ITEM), one eight-row TABLE, exact book counts, the HEAD-encoded first prohemium line, four double-stanza LG, and the source-layer boundaries through two unnumbered errata pages.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English item CME00114.
evidence.summary: Official current item evidence for title, Palladius/Liddell contributor display, Berlin 1896 publication, complete content menu, public access, and U-M's public-domain / No Copyright-United States statement. Both item URLs returned HTTP 403 to unauthenticated command-line GET requests during the final link audit, so content and structure claims are also independently grounded in the checked-in XML and scanned edition.

evidence.citation: Mark Liddell, ed., The Middle-English Translation of Palladius De Re Rustica, Part I—Text (Berlin: E. Ebering, 1896).
evidence.locator: title p.; Temporary Preface; printed pp. 1-289; PDF pp. 7, 11-12, and nonblank text surfaces through p. 561

A. 369 correction and collation, editorial expansions and punctuation, complete indexed poem, appended collation, and page mapping. The scanned exemplar ends at printed p. 289 and omits the XML's two unnumbered errata pages.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Medieval Manuscripts in Oxford Libraries, MS. Add. A. 369.
evidence.summary: Current official evidence for the collateral correction/collation witness at fols. 14v-121v, Middle English verse form, 1440-1460 English production, losses, abrupt ending, Colchester provenance, Lodge edition, and Bodleian acquisition.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian one-image IIIF object for MS. Add. A. 369, UUID 61120deb-7b6b-409d-bd8d-461dc3432a00.
evidence.summary: The provider's complete published selected-image object contains only fol. 009v, before the target begins at fol. 14v, and therefore has zero target-work overlap. The exact manifest applies CC BY-NC 4.0 to the image.

evidence.citation: Barton Lodge, ed., Palladius on Husbondrie, EETS Original Series 52 and 72 (London, 1873 and 1879), with rhyme index edited by Sidney J. H. Herrtage.
evidence.locator: title PDF p. 7; preface and apparatus PDF pp. 9-40; transcription printed pp. 1-220 / PDF pp. 41-260; notes and indices through printed p. 387 / PDF p. 427
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for the editor's name Barton, Add. A. 369 as the former Colchester Castle manuscript, its incomplete Book XII and absent Book XIII, and the earlier EETS edition compared in Liddell's appendix.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0, records 1071 and 254 and associated witness and bibliography data.
evidence.locator: data/Records.xml record-1071 and record-254; data/MSSIndex.xml Duke Humfrey d.2 and Add. A.369; data/Bibliography.xml Liddell1896, Lodge1872-79, Howlett1977
evidence.summary: Versioned evidence for preferred title, incipits, IMEV/NIMEV 654, rhyme schemes, agriculture subject, component indexing, witness census and loci, and editions. Direct title pages override DIMEV's 'Burton Lodge' error, and direct XML/edition evidence establishes thirteen labeled books despite record 1071's twelve-book description.

open_questions.question: Can the anonymous translator's identity, precise translation place, or dialect region be established beyond the current England context and the reported Thomas Norton proposal?
next_steps: Consult Howlett 1977 and subsequent responses rather than promoting the catalog's reported proposal to known authorship.
Compare linguistic evidence across Duke Humfrey d. 2, Add. A. 369, and other indexed witnesses while separating authorial language from scribal spelling.
Keep Humfrey's Oxford book donation, witness production in England, and composition geography as distinct claims.

open_questions.question: Which Liddell exemplar supplied the two unnumbered errata pages encoded as PB REF 299-300, and where can those pages be imaged directly?
next_steps: Locate another complete physical or digital copy of Liddell 1896 and compare the leaves after printed p. 289.
Verify all thirty-eight encoded errata page by page against that exemplar.
Retain the cached Internet Archive scan as complete for its provider object but do not claim that it contains the errata leaves.

open_questions.question: Can the target fols. 14v-121v of MS. Add. A. 369 be obtained as a complete usable facsimile?
next_steps: Do not treat the catalog-linked one-image Digital Bodleian object as target coverage: its sole canvas is fol. 009v, before the work begins at fol. 14v.
Ask Bodleian Special Collections or Imaging Services about existing photography or a complete imaging order and exact reuse terms.
Do not substitute Duke Humfrey d. 2 images for Add. A. 369 when evaluating Liddell's corrections or Lodge's transcript.

notes: The primary path is repository XML to U-M encoding to Liddell's 1896 edition to Bodleian MS. Duke Humfrey d. 2. Add. A. 369 is a correction/collation witness, not Liddell's base.

Liddell's appendix additionally compares Barton Lodge's EETS edition with Add. A. 369; the edition title's 'formerly in Colchester Castle' describes that Bodleian witness, not a British Library manuscript.
Current foliation is split: Tabula x r-xviii v, prohemium xix r-xxi v, and main translation 1r-118v. Liddell's printed legacy foliation renumbers these runs and is preserved only as a crosswalk.

DIMEV record 254's separately indexed prohemium is modeled as an integral authorial part. Record 1071's twelve-book description conflicts with thirteen direct XML and printed headings; the direct edition governs.
Work discovery metrics remain raw encoding counts, with the nine non-L verse positions and four double-stanza LG elements disclosed rather than silently rewriting the XML.

The complete 304-canvas Digital Bodleian object is distinct from a competing three-canvas selected-image object with the same title; facsimile completeness is documented in the paired lineage manifest.
Rights, provider terms, complete-facsimile access, and reproduction routes are maintained layer by layer in the paired lineage manifest.
